description: Four U.S. states have ZERO rows in the high-yield review master
estimated_impact: Visible coverage hole — any user from AK ID MT WY sees no listings
recommended_fix: Add representative academic / public hospital systems for AK / ID / MT / WY in Queue 4
notes: AK: Providence Alaska Medical Center / Alaska Native Medical Center; ID: Saint Alphonsus / U Idaho WAMI; MT: Billings Clinic / Saint Vincent; WY: Wyoming Medical Center / Cheyenne Regional

description: 14+ states have only 1-2 rows in the high-yield set
estimated_impact: Thin coverage — many users see 0-1 listing per state
recommended_fix: Add 2-4 well-chosen institutions per thin-coverage state in Queue 4
notes: Thin states: ME DC NH VT ND SD NM NE OR UT CO IA AL AR

description: Massive backlog: 347 sourceProofScore=5 rows but only 5 active runtime cards (~70x ratio)
estimated_impact: The biggest single gap in the funnel; 347 → 25 → 7 → 5 → 0
recommended_fix: Promote rows from the 25-row READY_FOR_HUMAN_APPROVAL set into the staged-batch-3 runtime; do this BEFORE more screening
notes: **This is the single highest-leverage action in the whole project.** Yale New Haven / Mayo Saint Marys / Cleveland Clinic Main / NYP Columbia + Cornell / Johns Hopkins / Mount Sinai / Duke / Northwestern / Penn / NYU Langone Tisch / Mayo Phoenix + Jacksonville / Westchester / Methodist TX / Newark Beth Israel / Saint Barnabas / BronxCare / Englewood / Carilion / Grady Memorial / Indiana University Health Methodist / ChristianaCare are sitting waiting.

description: Active 5 cards include 3 US-only (Morristown / Overlook / Highland) + 2 IMG-relevant (CCF Mercy + Hillcrest); staged adds 1 IMG-relevant (UPMC) + 1 US-only (Lincoln) → 3 IMG / 4 US
estimated_impact: IMG-friendly card density on the public preview is low
recommended_fix: Queue 4 should bias toward known IMG-friendly large city safety-nets and academic systems with documented international visiting student programs
notes: Queue 4 candidates with IMG-eligibility signal should be flagged early

description: Caribbean named-partner pathways exist for SGU (Atlantic Health = Morristown + Overlook) but no other Caribbean school's affiliate list is in repo
estimated_impact: Ross / AUC / Saba / MUA / Avalon students see no pathway; only SGU is implicitly visible
recommended_fix: Per-school Caribbean affiliate harvesting in a future sprint; document as a gap now
notes: Out of scope for Queue 4 itself; flagged for a future denominator sprint

description: Q3a includes 126 VSLO-remaining institutions; subset already in high-yield master
estimated_impact: VSLO-participation is partly captured but not exhaustive
recommended_fix: Queue 4 should not duplicate VSLO-only institutions already in Q3a

description: Many institutions document "observership" rather than "elective" — these are different categories
estimated_impact: Audience confusion if observerships and electives are mixed in card copy
recommended_fix: Continue surfacing this distinction at the curator-review stage; do not muddy Queue 4 by merging the two
notes: Already enforced via opportunity_type field in the runtime schema

description: Sub-internships specifically (vs general visiting electives) are a sub-class that is uneven
estimated_impact: Spec-specific sub-I info often hides under "advanced clinical experience"
recommended_fix: Note in Queue 4 candidate rows that sub-I-specific institutions are an acceptable subset

description: T7 lane carries the canonical 347-row workbench but Mac-local does NOT have those CSVs
estimated_impact: Future code on Mac-local can reference T7 but builds a fragile cross-lane dependency
recommended_fix: Either transplant the canonical workbench to Mac-local OR formalize T7 as canonical for these source lists
notes: Cosmetic for screening but blocking for any Mac-local-only validator that needs to read the workbench

description: Staged 7-card runtime has been built but is NOT imported by any app code; activation gated by separate authorized sprint
estimated_impact: Even if Queue 4 produces 100 new candidates the funnel still narrows to ~7 cards
recommended_fix: Authorize a runtime activation sprint to flip staged → active on a separate branch / preview only; keep production untouched
notes: User has consistently held this back; respect that

description: Correction intake endpoint exists (/api/usce/corrections) but is env-flag disabled by default; /contact UI does not parse listing_id/ref
estimated_impact: Reports filed via the live contact form would lose listing context
recommended_fix: B-001/B-002/B-003 deferred; queue this as the LAST gate before any production exposure
notes: The intake-and-validator stack is built; UI wiring is the only missing piece

description: No public route currently displays the staged 7-card runtime
recommended_fix: Activation is a separate later sprint

description: Active 5 cards are multispecialty; specialty-specific subi/elective coverage is uneven across queues
estimated_impact: Specialty-search UX would surface this; not a blocker today

description: Many large city public hospital systems (NYC H+H Bellevue / LAC+USC / Cook County / Parkland Dallas / Harborview Seattle / Boston Medical Center / SF General-ZSFG / Grady Memorial / Jackson Memorial) likely under-represented relative to AMCs
estimated_impact: Public hospitals are often IMG-relevant; gap reduces audience match
recommended_fix: Queue 4 should explicitly include public-safety-net systems for major metros

description: Some major AMCs not in Q1-Q3: e.g. Stanford / UCSF / UCSD / UCLA / Vanderbilt / WashU / Emory / UMich / Pitt-UPMC system index page
estimated_impact: Top AMCs missing makes the platform look incomplete to professional reviewers
recommended_fix: Queue 4 should include high-priority AMC gaps
